children.name: Alauda
children.description: The Skylark's sustained spiralling song-flight — rising to a tiny dot 300 m above Swedish farmland — is one of the most celebrated sounds in northern European natural history. Populations have fallen by over half across Europe in 50 years due to changes in farming.

children.name: Alauda arvensis
children.description: Its sustained, rippling song delivered from a hovering speck high above the fields is one of the most celebrated sounds of the Swedish farmland summer. Populations have more than halved in Europe in fifty years.

children.name: Lullula
children.description: Shorter-tailed larks of sandy open country with scattered trees.

children.name: Lullula arborea
children.description: A short-tailed lark with a sweet, yodelling, downward-cascading song, favouring sandy heaths and clearings with scattered trees. It reaches the northern edge of its range in southern Sweden.

children.name: Eremophila
children.description: Larks with striking black-and-yellow facial patterns.

children.name: Eremophila alpestris
children.description: A lark with a striking black-and-yellow face pattern and tiny feathered 'horns', breeding on Arctic and alpine tundra. Swedish birds frequent the high fells, with wintering flocks on coastal shores.
